display.purpose: Cormorant Garamond serif for page/hero and tenant names only.

numeric.purpose: Space Mono tabular figures for every metric (.tnum).

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Ease-out for state transitions, 150-250ms. No bounce.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The page's single main commitment (Start free, See my estimate).

components.name: Field Input
components.description: Numeric/text field; metrics use tabular figures.

components.name: Panel Card
components.description: Surface panel lifted off Ink by tonal step + hairline border. No shadow.

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Health status pill — tints toward its hue and always carries a word, never color alone.

narrative.overview: OutFront Data reads like the instrument panel of a well-run business after dark - a calm, low-glow surface where a tired owner can glance once and know whether they are safe, profitable, and what is pressuring them. Credible like a good ledger, warm like a serif headline, never flashy. It rejects generic SaaS, bank-scare screens, over-technical accounting software, and the flashy AI toy. Density is earned: calm on a phone, dense on desktop. Color is rationed so a single copper accent or health signal carries real weight.
keyCharacteristics: Matte near-black canvas; warmth from copper + serif, never a tinted warm background.
Monospaced tabular figures for every number.
One copper accent, rationed; health green/yellow/red is a separate labeled vocabulary.
Serif display headings over a clean sans body.
Restraint as default; cool factor from precision and motion, not decoration.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Copper appears on ~<=10% of any screen - primary action, current selection, the single most important figure. If two things are copper, one is wrong.

rules.name: The Status-Is-Not-Decoration Rule
rules.body: Green/yellow/red belong to financial health only and never carry meaning by color alone - always pair with a word, number, or icon. Health Yellow and Copper Soft share a hex; never let an accent read as a watch status.

rules.name: The Quiet-Ground Rule
rules.body: The background stays Ink. Warmth is copper, serif, and data - never a cream/sand/warm canvas.

rules.name: The Every-Number-Is-Mono Rule
rules.body: All metrics use Space Mono tabular figures. A dollar amount in body sans is a bug.

rules.name: The Serif-Stays-In-Headings Rule
rules.body: Cormorant is for display and titles only - never buttons, labels, inputs, or table data.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat. Separation via the Ink->Surface tonal step and 1px Line border. No drop shadows, glows, or glassmorphism.

dos: Do show the heartbeat first - feedback before every source is connected.
Do render every number in Space Mono via .tnum.
Do pair every green/yellow/red signal with a label, number, or icon and verify AA contrast.
Do ration copper to ~one accent per view.
Do separate panels with the Ink->Surface tonal step and a 1px Line border.
Do make each industry feel modeled, not the same dashboard relabeled.
Do write the money in plain language; teach while diagnosing.
Do keep motion 150-250ms conveying state, and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't make it look like generic SaaS - no cream/sand bg, no gradient hero-metric, no identical card grid.
Don't use glassmorphism, decorative shadows/glows, or gradient text.
Don't use a colored border-left/right stripe as a card/alert accent.
Don't convey financial status with color alone.
Don't let Copper and Health Yellow (both #D9A35E) blur roles.
Don't tint the Ink ground toward warm.
Don't put the serif in buttons, labels, inputs, or table data.
Don't trap the owner in setup before showing value.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer.
